title: Handling Asynchronous Errors
description: Catch errors in async code and avoid unhandled rejections.

slides.title: Error-First Callbacks 📞
Promises and .catch() 🤝
slides.content:
With promises, use `.catch()`:
```javascript
fs.promises.readFile('file.txt')
  .then(data => console.log(data))
  .catch(err => console.error('Error:', err));
```

If you don't attach a `.catch()`, you'll get an **unhandled rejection** (which can crash future Node versions).

slides.title: Async/Await with try/catch 🥅
Global Unhandled Rejections 🌍

quizData.question: How do you handle errors in an async function using async/await?
quizData.explanation: `try/catch` works with `await` because it turns asynchronous code into synchronous-looking blocks.
